mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly
mission.description: Dragonfly is a modern in-memory data store that serves as a drop-in replacement for Redis and Memcached, designed for high throughput and low resource consumption. It is ideal for applications requiring efficient caching and data retrieval with minimal latency. Key trade-offs include compatibility with existing Redis/Memcached APIs versus the need for migration to a new system.

steps.title: Add Dragonfly Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Dragonfly Helm repository to your local Helm client, allowing you to install Dragonfly using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dragonfly https://dragonflydb.github.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Dragonfly Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Dragonfly to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml

```

> This command installs the Dragonfly CRDs.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dragonfly application in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace --version 1.37.0

```

> Installs Dragonfly in the 'dragonfly' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Dragonfly Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Dragonfly custom resource to deploy an instance of Dragonfly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This command creates a Dragonfly instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dragonfly Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dragonfly deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> This command lists all Dragonfly instances in the 'dragonfly' namespace.
> This command lists all pods in the 'dragonfly' namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dragonfly
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dragonfly installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources may remain.

```bash

helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly

kubectl delete namespace dragonfly

```

> Uninstalls the Dragonfly release.
> Deletes the Dragonfly namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade Dragonfly to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly -o json > dragonfly-backup.json

helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --version 1.37.0

kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly

```

> Backup the current Dragonfly configuration.
> Upgrades Dragonfly to the specified version.
> Verify that the Dragonfly instance is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly -o json > dragonfly-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dragonfly instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dragonfly custom resource may not have been created or is in a failed state.

**Fix:**
Check the Dragonfly custom resource with 'kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly' and ensure it is created successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Dragonfly'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dragonfly pods may not be running or are in a crash loop.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n dragonfly' to check the pod status and 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dragonfly' for logs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the Dragonfly pods.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the Dragonfly deployment and ensure the cluster has sufficient resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions for Dragonfly may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the CRD installation command: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new caching algorithms and improved compatibility with Redis commands.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dragonfly instance listed with 'kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly' and the pods running with 'kubectl get pods -n dragonfly'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
